user_message: I have this support ticket from Wide World Importers that needs investigate the decline. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: executive brief Discussion - Wide World Importers
Date: 2024-03-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into executive brief.

[00:08:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us.

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for security compliance is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:17:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:19:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): On the executive brief front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:23:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:28:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:30:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:32:00] Ryan Kowalski (Wide World Importers): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is data residency.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF47-N trap (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak); produces complete Decision log with speaker attribution, action distribution by role, stakeholder map delta, MSX updat
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF47-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF47-N failure mode (Follow-up-and-decisions module assumes named speakers when transcript has 'Speak)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct executive brief decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
